title: 4.2 Tree Traversal
subtitle: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and level-order traversal methods

content.introduction: Tree traversal defines systematic node visiting orders and is essential for analysis, serialization, and expression processing.

sections.title: 4.2.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define preorder, inorder, postorder, and level-order traversal goals. Explain how visit order changes interpretation.
examples: List traversal orders for a small binary tree
Explain why inorder yields sorted output in BST
Map postorder to safe deletion order

sections.title: 4.2.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe recursive and iterative implementations using stacks/queues. Show state transitions for each traversal type.
examples: Implement iterative preorder with a stack
Implement level-order traversal with a queue

sections.title: 4.2.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Traversal is O(n) time; auxiliary space depends on recursion depth or explicit data structures. Discuss practical implementation choices.
examples: Compare recursion depth on balanced vs skewed trees
Evaluate stack memory usage in DFS traversals
Choose traversal order by task objective

keyTakeaways: Understand the meaning of major traversal orders
